frame_analyses.description: Two laparoscopic instruments positioned over the suture board with suture visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial instrument positioning, suture appears to be pre-loaded

frame_analyses.description: Instruments manipulating the suture, preparing for needle loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument control during needle preparation

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned near the drain marks, suture threading preparation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise positioning for suture placement through drain marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning of knot tying sequence, creating first throw with instruments
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot formation technique being initiated

frame_analyses.description: Continuing knot formation, working on subsequent throws
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth instrument movements during throw creation

frame_analyses.description: Completing final throw of the knot sequence
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw being secured with good tension control

knot_assessments.notes: Proper double throw surgeon's knot as first throw
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw with hand switching

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well approximated with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Solid performance with proper surgeon's knot formation, appropriate hand switching between throws, and good overall technique. The knot appears secure and the drain closure is adequate.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
Work on slightly faster instrument movements to reduce overall time
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot formation on first throw
